---
title: "Document Operations"
---

## Importing A Document

This section explains how to upload and import supported files using Hyland for Salesforce into the Hyland Content Innovation Cloud (Hyland's content repository). For a list of supported files, see Supported Files.

You can upload and import a file and add it as a document to Hyland for Salesforce in the context of an existing custom or standard Salesforce object's record.

Note: You can only upload and import files up to a certain file size (by default, the maximum file size is 5,000MB). Contact your system administrator if you need to upload a file larger than the maximum file size. For detailed instructions, see Configuring the System Settings.
When you import a supported file, Hyland for Salesforce indexes the file and creates a new document. You can do one of the following:

- browse to a file location on your computer and select a file to import
- drag and drop a file onto the Capture Documents window

After you provide the necessary information required to provide context to the files (properties or metadata) that identify the document uniquely, you can import the document into the Hyland Content Innovation Cloud.

To import a document:

1.  Open a web browser and log on to the Salesforce environment with your credentials. The Home page is displayed.
2.  On the Salesforce Record Detail page, navigate to the section where Hyland for Salesforce is added.
3.  Log in to the application if you aren't authenticated already.
4.  To import a document, do one of the following:  Note: Depending on your system's configuration, you may be able to access the Upload and Import window, the Document List window, or both. Contact your system administrator if you need access to a window you do not have access to. For specific instructions to add these components, see Adding Components.
-
Select the file you want to import from your computer, drag the file to the Capture Documents window and drop it in the Drag and drop to import files area.

![Capture Documents window](data:image/gif;base64,)
-
In the Document List window, click Import Documents .

![Import Documents button](data:image/gif;base64,)
After you import one or more files, the Capture Documents window is displayed.
5.  In the Capture Documents window, on the right-hand pane, enter the properties (or metadata) associated with a document by modifying the fields as required. These properties and their values provide context to the content in the files that you imported.  Note: You can modify a field or change properties based on configuration changes made in the Hyland Content Innovation Cloud and through the Solution Configurations section within Hyland for Salesforce. These changes are typically made by your system administrator. Please contact your system administrator if you require help with these configurations. For detailed instructions, see the following:
-
Creating and Deploying Content Types

-
Configuring User Groups and Roles

-
Configuring the Solution Configurations

6.  After you have modified the fields, click Upload to upload a single file or click Upload All to upload multiple files you imported.  Note: To import and add more files, click Add More Files.  One or more files that you selected are imported.

## Obtaining The Viewer Url

While configuring the settings on the Application Configuration page, you must enter a value for the Viewer URL field. This section contains information on how to configure and obtain the value for the Viewer URL.

Each document captured using Hyland for Salesforce contains a unique ID. This unique ID is used to form a standard URL for each document displayed within the application.

To obtain the value for the Viewer URL, you must use the Hyland Automate Administration application and configure the value.Note: For more information on how to use the Hyland Experience Studio Administration application, see the Admin Application chapter in the Hyland Automate Administration documentation.
To obtain the Viewer URL:

1.  Log in to the Studio Administration App.
2.  From the left pane, expand Devops and select Application Instances.
3.  Under the Application name column, search for hfs-configurations and select it.
4.  Under the Workspace column, select Hyland For Salesforce HX Viewer and right-click it. A drop-down menu is displayed.
5.  From the drop-down, select Copy link address and paste the copied value in a plain-text editor.
6.  Append the following to the link address you copied:
/#/default/documents/{doc_id}

For example, if the link address you copied is https://example.com, then the complete link will be

https://example.com/#/default/documents/{doc_id}

Note: When the list of one or more documents are displayed in the application, the {doc_id} is populated with the unique ID for a document to form a URL specific to each document. Users can click this URL to open a document in a new tab of their browser.
7.  Copy the entire link and paste it in the Viewer URL field on the Application Configurations page.
For more information on how to configure this field and other settings on the Application Configurations page, see Configuring the Application Configurations.

## Removing The Document List View Component

This sections explains how to remove the Document List View component or hfsRecordDLPage.

Before uninstalling the Hyland for Salesforce Managed package, make sure to remove the Document List View component (hfsRecordDLPage) from the record detail pages of any Salesforce objects where it was configured during installation. For instructions on adding the Document List View (hfsRecordDLPage), see Adding the Document List View Component.
To remove the Document List View component from the record detail page:

1.  Log on to Salesforce with your credentials. The Home page is displayed.
2.  From the top-right, click Setup. The Home page is displayed in a new browser tab.
3.  In the left pane, select Platform Tools | Objects and Fields | Object Manager.  The Object Manager page is displayed.
4.  On the Object Manager page, navigate to the Salesforce object for which you created a mapping.
5.  To edit the lightning record page for this Salesforce object, click Edit.
6.  Search for hfsRecordDLPage and click the bin or delete icon from the top right corner.
7.  Click Save.

## Searching And Filtering Documents

This section shows you how to set filters so you can view only the documents captured using Hyland for Salesforce that match your specified criteria.

After you capture documents using Hyland for Salesforce, you can view them in the Document List window. You can also configure and apply filters to show only the documents that match the criteria you choose.
To search and filter documents:

1.  Open a web browser and log on to the Salesforce environment with your credentials. The Home page is displayed.
2.  On the Salesforce Record Detail page, navigate to the section where Hyland for Salesforce is added.
3.  Log in to the application if you aren't authenticated already. The Document List window for the selected Salesforce object is displayed.
This page displays a list of captured documents, columns and column names, total number of records, pages in the Document List window, and filtering criteria to sort and display documents.

Note: These details are shown based on the settings configured through the Solution Configurations and Application Configurations sections within Hyland for Salesforce. These changes are typically made by your system administrator. Please contact your system administrator if you require help with these configurations. For detailed instructions, see the following:
-
Configuring the Application Configurations

-
Configuring the Solution Configurations

4.  In the Quick Find text box, enter text or characters that you want to locate in the list of displayed document. A list of documents containing only the text or characters you entered and occuring in any of the columns and keywords corresponding to these documents is displayed in the Document List window.
For example, if you entered example, all documents containing this value in the column names as well as the keywords, such as Document Name, File Name, is displayed.

Note:
-
The search is restricted to keyword values only and does not search within the contents of captured documents.

-
Only columns and keywords and their values that are preconfigured are displayed in the Document List window. Configuring keywords and their values are typically done by your system administrator. Please contact your system administrator if you require help with these. For detailed instructions, see Configuring the Solution Configurations.

Tip: To organize your view, click the arrow next to any column to sort documents in ascending or descending order. You can also drag columns to rearrange them as needed.
5.  To specify filtering criteria that determines which documents are displayed in the Document List window, click the funnel-shaped icon to the left of the Import to Hyland for Salesforce button. The Filter section is displayed.
6.  In the Filter section, you can do the following:
- Add a rule
- Add rule logic
7.  To add a rule, click Add Rule.
1.  From the Field drop-down list, select an option.
These options include the column names displayed in the Document List window.

2.  From the Operator drop-down list, select one of the following options:
| Option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Equals | Only documents with keywords that match the text you enter for Value are displayed in the results. |
| Does Not Equals | Only documents with keywords that do not begin with the text you enter for Value are displayed in the results. |
| Starts With | Only documents with keywords that begin with the text you enter for Value are displayed in the results. |
| Ends With | Only documents with keywords that end with the text you enter for Value are displayed in the results. |
| Contains | Only documents with keywords that contain the text you enter for Value are displayed in the results. |

3.  In the Value text box, enter a value.
This value and the selected operator determine the search and filter criteria. For example, if you chose Document ID as the Field, Contains as the Operator, and entered example as the Value, the results will show all documents with example anywhere in their Document ID.

Note:
-
You can add multiple rules. To add multiple rules, repeat the steps. By default, all rules are combined with AND as the Operator and are applied while filtering documents.

-
To use a combination of rules, such as 1 AND 2 OR 3, you must use a rule logic.

4.  Click Apply Filter. The Document List window displays all documents that match all the rules you configured.
8.  To add a rule logic, click Add Rule Logic.
This enables you to select a combination of rules from the two or more rules you configured.

A text box is displayed.
9.  In the text box, you can add AND or OR between the rule numbers, depending on how you want the rules to work together. Then click Apply Filter.
-
For example, if you added two rules and used OR, you can change to AND to make both rules required. The rule logic will be 1 AND 2 and both rules are applied.

-
If you added three rules and used a mix of AND and OR, you can change them to explore different combinations.The possible rule logics can be:

| Combination | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 1 AND 2 AND 3 | All three rules are applied while filtering. |
| 1 OR 2 OR 3 | Either of the three rules are applied while filtering. |
| 1 AND 2 OR 3 | Rule 1 and 2 are applied, and then rule 3 is applied. Documents are shown in the results if both rules 1 and 2 are matched or rule 3 is matched. |
| 1 OR 2 AND 3 | Rule 2 and 3 are applied, and then rule 1 is applied. Documents are shown in the results if both rules 2 and 3 are matched or rule 1 is matched. |

Note: To prioritize a specific group of rules when applying filters, enclose that group in brackets. For example, if you’ve set up the rule logic as:1 AND 2 AND 3 OR 4 and you want 2 AND 3 to be evaluated first, add brackets around them: 1 AND (2 AND 3) OR 4. This ensures that rule 2 and 3 are applied first before combining with the others.
The Document List window displays all documents that match the rules you configured.

## Viewing Documents

This section explains how to view documents captured using Hyland for Salesforce after uploading and importing supported files.

After you capture documents using Hyland for Salesforce, you can view them in the Document List window. You can also apply filters to see only the documents that match the criteria you set.
To view documents:

1.  Open a web browser and log on to the Salesforce environment with your credentials. The Home page is displayed.
2.  On the Salesforce Record Detail page, navigate to the section where Hyland for Salesforce is added.
3.  Log in to the application if you aren't authenticated already. The Document List window for the selected Salesforce object is displayed.
This page displays a list of captured documents, columns and column names, total number of records, pages in the Document List window, and filtering criteria to sort and display documents.

![Document List window](data:image/gif;base64,)  Note: These details are shown based on the settings configured through the Solution Configurations and Application Configurations sections within Hyland for Salesforce. These changes are typically made by your system administrator. Please contact your system administrator if you require help with these configurations. For detailed instructions, see the following:
-
Configuring the Application Configurations

-
Configuring the Solution Configurations

4.  Select a document that you want to view and click the down arrow at the end of the row.   ![Row and adjoining down arrow selected in the Document List window](data:image/gif;base64,)   A drop-down menu is displayed.
5.  From the drop-down menu, select View. The selected document is displayed in a new browser tab.
